title: galium commune var. intertextum
intro: Galium commune var. intertextum, also known as cleavers, is a perennial herb native to Europe, Asia, and North America, with a sticky stem and leaves that resemble tiny hooks. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating skin conditions, wounds, and infections. The health benefits of cleavers include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and support the body's natural detoxification processes. Cleavers can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote skin health, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
